content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE
        Router reason: This is durable architectural guidance and process rationale from documentation, suitable as reusable note memory rather than a user-specific claim/task.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create a task unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DIRECTION; Read path uses need-gating, retrieval modes, and explicit v3 budget caps; Section 12 specifies that memory retrieval should be triggered only for qualifying question types, then use mode selection (claim-first, note-first, mixed, evidence-first/fallback) and explicit per-mode caps before composing answers with verification/conflict/scope safeguards.; scope=01. Architecture / 12. Read path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<1> DECISION; v3 memory architecture uses four axes to prevent transcript-dump or brittle-fact failure modes; In v3, memory is structured along four axes—Representation (Source/Claim/Note/Profile/Task/Episode), Lifecycle (write→reconcile→consolidate→repair→retain/archive/delete), Retrieval policy (claim-first/note-first/mixed/evidence-first with bounded budgets), and Scope and lineage (scope_json, scope_text, source refs, origin note, memory runs). The document states these axes are necessary to avoid degenerating into a transcript dump or brittle fact store.; scope=v3 memory architecture for the current project
- note hot-path:note:<2> DOC_DIGEST; Dual write model: semantic and maintenance paths in 01. Architecture; The architecture defines two semantic write paths (Direct structured path for safely normalizable items, and Note path for important but not-yet-hard-fact context) plus two maintenance paths (Consolidation to promote mature notes and Repair to fix memory degradation).; scope=01. Architecture section '3. Основная модель записи' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<3> DECISION; Truth state and Retrieval state must remain separate; Section 8 establishes that truth status and retrieval hotness are independent dimensions: truth state tracks knowledge validity (with different allowed states for claims vs notes), while retrieval state tracks indexing/hot-path placement (active/warm/cold/archived/deleted). Items can be truth-resolved or superseded yet remain warm for rationale or historical evidence retrieval.; scope=01. Architecture section 8 ('Truth state и retrieval state') in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<4> DIRECTION; Claim vs Note write policy in 01. Architecture; Section 7 defines a decision rule: write Claim for explicit, normalizable, precise-answer-ready information comparable by predicate policy; write Note for already important retrieval context that is too contextual or too early for hard facts; and write both when a source contains explicit facts/tasks plus rationale or direction.; scope=01. Architecture section 7 ('Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`) in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<5> DECISION; Memory repair is a separate background workflow from consolidation; Section 14 establishes `Repair` as a distinct background workflow focused on memory health issues: duplicate notes/claims, contradictory active items, profile drift, stale task states, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage. It is explicitly separated from consolidation: consolidation enriches memory, while repair restores/maintains health.; scope=01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<6> DIRECTION; Memory update flow must include retrieval/reconciliation and policy actions, not append-only writes; The architecture sets four mandatory write-time tasks: decide whether to write memory, retrieve similar/conflicting items, convert input to typed candidates, and apply policy actions (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicts, task explosion, and summary spam.; scope=01. Architecture section 9 (update should not be append-only)
- note hot-path:note:<7> DIRECTION; Note maturity lifecycle prevents Note summary-store degradation; Section 13 defines a maturity lifecycle for `Note` (`fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`) to prevent notes from degrading into a summary-store. Promotion signals include retrieval usage, multi-source confirmation, conflict-free survival, time without supersede, and recurring rationale value; demotion/closure signals include explicit supersede, stale scope, non-usage, and weak evidence support.; scope=01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<8> DOC_DIGEST; Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture in 01. Architecture; Section 5 defines five storage tiers with distinct roles: Tier A for compact in-prompt context, Tier B for typed long-term records, Tier C for semantic notes, Tier D for immutable raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes and filters.; scope=01. Architecture section '5. Storage tiers' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<9> DOC_DIGEST; Memory layer goals for persistent, explainable, and maintainable agent memory; The architecture defines a memory layer that must persist across sessions, store facts/preferences/tasks/rationale/evolving plans, support knowledge updates, preserve provenance, avoid making every turn permanent, and support aging, archiving, and repair.; scope=01. Architecture section '1. Цель системы' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<10> DIRECTION; v3 structured scope requires both scope_text and scope_json with mandatory envelope fields; Section 6 sets a design direction that v3 stores both human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; the minimum `scope_json` includes `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation predictability and reduce retrieval overgeneralization/conflicts.; scope=01. Architecture section '6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
- note hot-path:note:<11> DIRECTION; Write path in 01. Architecture is an 8-step transactional pipeline; Section 11 defines a fixed write workflow: persist source, route, retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, extract via specialized prompts, reconcile with existing memory, apply all changes in one transaction, and schedule maintenance jobs.; scope=01. Architecture / 11. Write path in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=01. Architecture; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<0>; type=existing; name=01. Architecture; reason=Document title exactly matches existing DOCUMENT entity.
- mention=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<1>; type=existing; name=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md; reason=Document source path exactly matches existing FILE entity.
- mention=v3; action=LINK_EXISTING; id=entity:<2>; type=existing; name=v3; reason=In this namespace context, 'v3' refers to the existing 'v3 memory architecture' project.
- mention=fast path; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=CONCEPT; name=Fast path (memory execution); reason=Distinct reusable architectural concept in the target section with no existing matching entity.
- mention=slow path; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=CONCEPT; name=Slow path (memory execution); reason=Distinct reusable architectural concept in the target section with no existing matching entity.
- mention=typed handoff objects; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Typed handoff objects; reason=Explicitly emphasized architectural concept; no safe existing candidate found.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=project:<0>|agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md|Retention / forgetting|Optional fast/slow execution path
        text_queries=v3 forgetting policy downgrade retrieval state archive soft delete hard delete|forgetting triggers namespace policy age low use_count legal privacy explicit user forget delete command|fast path slow path typed handoff objects escalation gate conflict resolution note consolidation memory repair
        predicate_hints=has_constraint|believes

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:5830e695351ef9d0d5fe11e08b843471] EXTERNAL
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting .. 16. Optional fast/slow execution path
Document lines: 509-546

## 15. Retention / forgetting

В `v3` forgetting — не случайное “пусть старое реже ищется”, а policy.

Минимальные действия:
- downgrade retrieval state
- archive
- soft delete
- hard delete by request

Минимальные триггеры:
- namespace policy
- age
- low use_count
- legal/privacy request
- explicit user forget/delete command

## 16. Optional fast/slow execution path

Это уже не обязательный MVP, но хороший следующий шаг.

### Fast path
- user-facing
- lower latency
- cheap retrieval
- cheap draft
- escalation gate

### Slow path
- stronger model / worker
- conflict resolution
- answer verification
- note consolidation
- memory repair

### Важный момент
Между fast и slow path лучше гонять **typed handoff objects**, а не свободный текст.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
